text.title: Evaluation and Deployment
text.summary: Post-training evaluation with LM Evaluation Harness, GGUF export for Ollama, vLLM server setup, and TGI Docker deployment with common pitfalls.

rule.statement: Run lm-eval on MMLU before and after fine-tuning to detect catastrophic forgetting early — a drop of more than 5 points on MMLU indicates the model has lost general capabilities.
rule.rationale: Fine-tuning on narrow task data reduces general benchmark performance; tracking MMLU delta quantifies the forgetting cost and gates whether the specialized gain is worth it.

rule.statement: Never use perplexity on training data as an eval metric — always compute on held-out data from the same distribution.
rule.rationale: Training perplexity approaches 0 as the model memorizes the training set; it is not a quality signal. Only held-out perplexity measures generalization.

rule.statement: lm-eval benchmarks take 2-8 hours on a 7B model — dispatch async and await completion signal; do not poll in a tight loop.
rule.rationale: Tight polling wastes CPU and produces no value during long-running evaluation; use file-based completion signals or webhook notifications.

rule.statement: Use q4_k_m quantization for Ollama deployment — good balance of quality and file size; use q8_0 only if perplexity difference is measurable on your eval set.
rule.rationale: q4_k_m is the community standard for local serving; q8_0 doubles file size with marginal quality improvement for most tasks.

example.code: # Unsloth: export to GGUF directly from training model.save_pretrained_gguf("model_gguf", tokenizer, quantization_method="q4_k_m")
example.note: Unsloth handles the merge-and-quantize pipeline in one step.

rule.statement: Always write GGUF files to a persistent volume path — never to /tmp on a cloud instance that terminates after training.
rule.rationale: Cloud GPU instances (RunPod, Lambda) terminate and lose /tmp on shutdown; writing to /tmp produces a lost model after an expensive training run.

rule.statement: Merge LoRA adapter before deploying to vLLM — pass the merged model directory, not the PEFT adapter path.
rule.rationale: vLLM requires a standard HF model; providing a PEFT adapter path causes a silent hang or error at startup.

rule.statement: Set --tensor-parallel-size to match available GPU count exactly; a mismatch causes a silent hang at vLLM startup.
rule.rationale: vLLM uses NCCL for tensor parallelism; wrong GPU count causes an NCCL init hang that appears as a startup freeze with no error message.

text.reference: Ollama Modelfile for LLaMA-3 chat template
